patterns.label: Azure DevOps Notification Triage
patterns.llmRationale: ~198 emails in window, heavily skewed to machine-generated ADO notifications (Task, Bug, Requirement, Feature, Epic prefixes). 30 occurrences/week at 45min total = highest time-sink in email. Fully parseable: subject prefix, work item type, assigned team are deterministic. Ideal for auto-classification and digest routing. Chain head for DevOps-Passive-Intake pipeline.

patterns.label: Agent Evaluation Framework Guidance (Recurring Expert Ask)
patterns.llmRationale: Single PM repeatedly queried by engineers, PMs, and customer success about eval-driven development, trajectory evaluation, scenario libraries, CI/CD integration, and VoC. 7 occurrences/week across 3+ roles = Expert Scaling Bottleneck (BSI HIGH). FAQ answers already structured -- eval scenario templates, CSV sets, stepwise evaluation concepts -- highly convertible to advisory skill.

patterns.label: Signal-to-Sync-to-Artifact Workflow Chain
patterns.llmRationale: Explicitly named three-step pipeline observed 4x: (1) Email thread raises blocker, (2) Focused meeting created, (3) Loop artifact captures decisions/open questions/follow-ups. Cross-source (email+meeting+doc), named by users themselves. Strong indicator of a structural workflow ready for automation. Chain head for multiple downstream artifacts. Named pipeline bonus applies.

patterns.label: Meeting Transcript to Summary and Deck Packaging
patterns.llmRationale: After meetings, participants independently re-package content for different audiences (ROB decks, exec briefings, customer follow-ups). Transformation is deterministic: meeting transcript -> audience-specific artifact. Sprint demos and builder meetings explicitly reference send-deck and automating-ROB-deck. Chain head amplifying weekly-status-report-generation and eval-results-reporting downstream.

patterns.label: Weekly Executive Status Update Manual Assembly
patterns.llmRationale: Weekly/exec/customer updates follow a highly consistent structure (what changed, risks, blockers, asks) but recreated manually each week. Structure is standardized in practice -- only generation is not. Cross-source (email + Loop doc), multi-participant, Standardization Gap Index HIGH. 3+ participants and cross-source elevate priority despite low occurrence count.

patterns.label: PRD and Spec Creation from Scratch (Parallel Teams)
patterns.llmRationale: Multiple teams independently generate PRDs, design docs, and task plans using similar sections (goals, non-goals, risks, KPIs, alternatives) but different tools/formats. Camp AIR standups repeatedly describe teams starting PRDs from scratch. Classic Parallel Creation Gap (SGI HIGH) -- same conceptual artifact, duplicated effort, divergent formats. Template scaffolding skill would standardize and reduce ramp time.

patterns.label: Agent Architecture and Identity Explanation (Repeated Questions)
patterns.llmRationale: Recurring pattern: people ask how things work under the hood -- Entra Agent IDs, registry behavior, Conditional Access, licensing. PM consistently translates ambiguous platform behavior into coherent mental models. 4 occurrences/week, 3 roles = Expert Scaling Bottleneck (BSI ELEVATED). Answers structured enough to document but require contextual judgment, capping automation score.

patterns.label: Sharing Technical Artifacts Across Tools
patterns.llmRationale: High-frequency (10x/week) pattern of sharing GitHub repos, SharePoint files, Loop pages, Aka.ms links as working context. Usually shared as context or building blocks, not just FYIs. Cross-tool scatter with deterministic link resolution. Highest frequency signal in Teams. Could be automated with smart context-packaging bundling related artifacts.

patterns.label: Security and Compliance Alert Triage
patterns.llmRationale: Subject pattern Action-Required File-Blocked repeats across different files from Office365/CISO/Purview. Workflow: quick open -> check file name -> decide action needed. Highly deterministic and machine-parseable. Low time per occurrence but high automation ceiling (89). Single participant reduces value score. Pairs well with ADO triage in a combined notification-router skill.

patterns.label: Internal Navigation and Routing Requests
patterns.llmRationale: Steady stream of navigation requests: finding recordings, docs, approvals; getting permissions unblocked; knowing who to loop in or escalate to. Users see this PM as someone who knows how the org works. Expert Scaling Bottleneck type. Harder to fully automate (requires org knowledge graph) but high deflection potential with a well-structured routing skill.

patterns.label: PowerPoint Deck Review and Circulation
patterns.llmRationale: Recurring pattern of reviewing and circulating exec-facing decks (MPR, RoB, Engineering Review, pitch decks). Multiple deck types, consistent review-and-route workflow. Cross-functional (PM, PM Lead, Engineering Lead). Automating deck summarization and routing decisions would reduce review cycle time significantly.

patterns.label: Loop Component Live PM Collaboration
patterns.llmRationale: Loop used repeatedly as live collaboration surface for PM reviews, planning, meeting notes, and ongoing decisions. 5 occurrences with 3 participants. Structural component of the Signal-to-Sync-to-Artifact pipeline. Downstream of meeting-output-packaging. Automating Loop page creation would accelerate the artifact step of the main workflow chain.

patterns.label: Daily Planning and Day-Prep Page Creation
patterns.llmRationale: Daily personal planning pages that triage inbox, frame decision lenses, and guide meeting engagement. Workflow: Prepare -> Engage -> Capture -> Re-prioritize. Single participant lowers value score but high personal ROI. 5x/week frequency is strong signal of a structural habit. Automation ceiling moderate -- requires connecting to calendar, email, and Teams context.

patterns.label: EvalCon Content Preparation and Coordination
patterns.llmRationale: EvalCon appears as deadline-driven milestone and forcing function for clarifying blockers, challenges, and process gaps. PM is pulled into content ownership and framing. Deadline amplification effect -- spikes 3+ related patterns simultaneously. Event Coordination archetype. Lower automation ceiling due to creative/strategic framing requirements.

patterns.label: Internal Broadcast and All-Hands Digest Consumption
patterns.llmRationale: BIC All Hands, Viva Engage digests, Copilot Champs weekly digests -- skimmed based on relevance. Single participant, passive consumption. Community Digest archetype. Lower value score due to passive nature and single-consumer pattern. Community Digest Overreach anti-pattern applies -- do not over-prioritize relative to chain-head patterns.

patterns.label: Weekly Status Report Loop Page Updates
patterns.llmRationale: Multiple weekly status report Loop files updated on a weekly cadence across workstreams (e.g., CAPE Compete Agent). Part of a larger recurring reporting pattern. Downstream of meeting-output-packaging and signal-sync-artifact chains. Low occurrence count but structural (weekly cadence, recurring). Pairs with weekly-exec-status-assembly.

patterns.label: Excel and CSV Planning and Eval Analytics
patterns.llmRationale: Several planning, analytics, and evaluation-oriented spreadsheets: MCS Customer Signals for Planning, M365Licenses.csv, eval templates. Programmatic and analysis artifacts consistent with PM work. Moderate automation ceiling -- data is structured but analysis requires domain context. Eval templates pair naturally with eval-framework-guidance.

patterns.label: Camp AIR Daily Standup (Immersion Program)
patterns.llmRationale: Camp AIR standups run Mon-Thu, 4x/week, 30min each. Program-bounded (not permanent) but regular while active. Immersion Cascade trigger: 8h of meeting time generates downstream spikes in PRD creation, artifact sharing, Loop docs, and skills discussions. Automating standup synthesis would reduce post-meeting overhead for the whole cohort. Apply 0.7x dampening for cascade children.

patterns.label: External Customer Eval and Demo Sessions
patterns.llmRationale: External meetings with customers for eval planning and demos. Rebuild-Per-Engagement pattern -- semi-custom materials created per external meeting. EDII contribution (50+ external domains in window). 2 occurrences with 3 participants. Medium automation ceiling -- customer context varies but prep structure (agenda, eval framework summary, demo script) is repeatable.

patterns.label: Skills Discovery and Agentic Workflow Automation Discussions
patterns.llmRationale: Recurring discussions about skills as reusable abstractions: skill detectors, skill creators, skill libraries, YAML definitions, WorkIQ mining for patterns. Builder-User Prototyping archetype -- PM is both building and using these tools. 4x/week with 3 collaborators. Low automation ceiling (strategic, creative) but high organizational learning value. Meta-pattern reinforcing the entire skill-detector mission.

candidateSkills.name: ado-notification-router
candidateSkills.description: Automatically classifies and digests Azure DevOps notification emails by work item type (Bug, Task, Requirement, Feature), team assignment, and urgency. Produces a prioritized daily digest instead of 30+ raw notification emails.
triggerExamples: I have too many ADO notification emails -- can you triage them?
Show me only the ADO bugs assigned to my team from today
Give me a digest of all new Requirements added this week
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Eliminates ~22.5h/week of manual notification scanning. Highest-frequency email pattern (30x/week). Machine-parseable subject lines and deterministic classification make this the easiest win with the highest ROI.

candidateSkills.name: eval-design-advisor
candidateSkills.description: Answers recurring questions about eval-driven agent development: trajectory evaluation, scenario library design, CI/CD integration, VoC capture, and pre/post-MVP eval placement. Acts as a scalable proxy for the eval SME bottleneck.
triggerExamples: How do I set up evals before my agent MVP?
What is the difference between trajectory and stepwise evaluation?
Help me design a scenario library for my Copilot agent
Where does eval fit in a CI/CD pipeline?
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Scales the eval SME expertise to 3+ roles asking 7+ times/week. Deflects BSI-HIGH bottleneck queries. Each automated response frees ~8-10 minutes of expert time while delivering consistent, high-quality guidance.

candidateSkills.name: signal-sync-artifact-orchestrator
candidateSkills.description: Guides users through the Signal-to-Sync-to-Artifact workflow: converts a blocker email thread into a meeting agenda, then captures the meeting output into a structured Loop artifact with decisions, open questions, and follow-ups.
triggerExamples: I have a blocker in this email thread -- help me turn it into a meeting
We just had a sync -- create a Loop page capturing our decisions
Thread raised an issue -- what is the fastest path to resolution?
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Automates a 3-step cross-tool workflow repeating 4x/week. Chain head for meeting-output-packaging and weekly-status downstream patterns. Named pipeline bonus: 40% more automatable than standalone patterns.

candidateSkills.name: weekly-status-report-generator
candidateSkills.description: Assembles weekly executive status updates from meeting notes, ADO signals, and Loop pages. Produces a consistent structure: what changed, risks, blockers, asks -- tailored for exec, team, and customer audiences.
triggerExamples: Generate my weekly status update for leadership
Pull together this week progress, blockers, and asks
Create the Friday RoB update from my meeting notes
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Eliminates manual re-assembly of a structure already standardized in practice. Cross-source (email + Loop). Standardization Gap Index HIGH -- multiple people recreate the same format independently. Downstream of signal-sync-artifact-pipeline.

candidateSkills.name: prd-spec-scaffolder
candidateSkills.description: Scaffolds PRDs, design docs, and task plans using a standardized structure (goals, non-goals, success metrics, risks, KPIs, alternatives, open questions). Works from a brief, a meeting transcript, or a conversation.
triggerExamples: Help me write a PRD for this feature
Scaffold a design doc from this email thread
Multiple teams are writing specs for the same thing -- create a canonical template
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Closes the Parallel Creation Gap where multiple teams independently create overlapping PRDs. SGI HIGH (Camp AIR standups show 4+ teams starting from scratch). Standardizing spec creation reduces ramp time and prevents format divergence.

candidateSkills.name: meeting-notes-action-extractor
candidateSkills.description: Transforms meeting transcripts into structured summaries: key decisions, action items with owners, open questions, and audience-specific artifacts (ROB deck bullets, exec briefing, customer follow-up email).
triggerExamples: Here is the meeting transcript -- extract the action items
Create a follow-up email from this Teams meeting
Package this transcript into the exec briefing format
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Chain head amplifying 3 downstream patterns (weekly-status, eval-reporting, Loop artifact creation). Automation score 88 -- transcript is machine-readable, output structure is deterministic. Saves ~2.67h/week and reduces post-meeting artifact lag.
